title: androcalva pulchella
intro: Androcalva pulchella is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, commonly known as the yellow bells or golden bells plant, native to the eastern coast of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its roots are known for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Androcalva pulchella include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Androcalva pulchella can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or topical cream, allowing for various methods of consumption and application.
